content:
We need more information. Most importantly:

- What error messages do you see? Please quote them verbatim and completely, and/or provide screen shots.

Also helpful:

- When did this start? - Who is your email provider? - Is it a POP3 or IMAP account? - What is your computer operating system and version? - What version of Thunderbird?
creator: rednoise

content:
The headers included in a forward depend on the menu setting <View | Headers>. If set to "Normal" you'll see minimal headers, usually just Subject, Date, From, and To. If set to "All" you'll get everything.

The setting also affects the headers shown when you view a message, but you have to scroll the header window down to see them, so you might not have noticed.
creator: rednoise

content: This is a server-side error message, indicating that your email provider has, for whatever reason, blocked you from sending email. It could be that they wrongly or rightly think you have been abusing the service by sending out spam, or it could be a temporary problem on their end. There's nothing you can do on your end except contact them to see what's going on.
creator: rednoise

content: Thank you for your rapid reply. Email provider has its own webmail client. All works fine using that, but now tith TBird. I really like TBird. Is there anything you can think of that might solve this?
creator: pheczko

content:
No, it can only be resolved on their end. It's your personal IP address that's blocked. When you use their web mail interface, the mail server interacts with their web server, not your home address.

It may go away on its own if there's a general server problem. If you feel like you've waited long enough, you must contact them for resolution.

If you use a VPN, that could very well be the issue. If so, can you disable it as a test?
creator: rednoise

content: Your computer is a different IP that your email provider. This URL looks of possible use, so I share it here: https://www.suped.com/knowledge/email-deliverability/sender-reputation/how-to-resolve-blacklisted-by-internal-reputation-service-email-bounces-from-small-isps Thunderbird itself is not the issue.
creator: davidsk

content: I don't know what effect this would have for you, but there is a setting found in <Settings | General | Incoming Mails | Change preferences for the app icon | App Icon Options button> where you can choose between "Count of unread messages" and "Count of new messages". Does changing that setting give you what you're looking for?
creator: rednoise

content:
Lin,

The Settings option you refer to no longer exists on TB 140.9.0esr - if it ever existed.

But I doubt if it would make any difference anyway.
creator: tamjk

content:
You're right, those settings are in Windows but not in Linux Mint or Ubuntu, although there are similar system settings in Mint but not Ubuntu. I tried it with Ubuntu 24 and Thunderbird 140.8.0esr and it works the way you describe. Then I let it update to Thunderbird 140.9.0esr and it appears to work the way you want.

I also noticed that if you have another device like another computer or a phone checking that mailbox, that can throw the new message count off in Thunderbird.

I'm thinking this more an Ubuntu thing than a Thunderbird thing, or a combination of both. If you think this is a bug, this article talks about how to report it:

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/file-bug-report-or-feature-request-mozilla
creator: rednoise
